creator: raimeetravel
caption:
How is this Japan’s least visited prefecture?! I loved my time in Tottori and I think this area deserves a spot on your Japan itinerary — especially if you want to get off the beaten path.

📝 I realize my inflection pronouncing Tottori might not be correct! But this is how I heard it while there! I’m always learning and trying my best! 

🎥 Check out more of my off the beaten path Japan itinerary recommendations >>> @raimeetravel

transcription.text: We are getting off the beaten path in Japan. This is Chitori. And it's one of Japan's least visited prefectures. I'm not sure how, because there are so many cool things to do here. So if you wanna go beyond Tokyo, Kyoto, and Osaka on your trip to Japan, this is what you're gonna do. Take a quick hour flight from Tokyo to Chitori and head straight to the sand museum. Each year is a different display. That's all sand. And I know it sounds weird, but it's so cool. And it's also right next to the sand dunes in Chitori, which are insane. They're right next to the ocean. I could not believe I was in Japan. There's also a ton of cute Pokemon stuff all over the city, and it's a great family-friendly destination too. You'll also find a ton of adorable cafes and really tasty breweries. There's also some very well-preserved towns like Kuroyoshi and a ton of beautiful ancient hillside temples. The Chitori prefecture is also home to one of the most radioactive springs in the world that is said to heal the mind and body. This is Misasa Onsen. As you can see, the Chitori prefecture is amazing. I hope I've convinced you to add it to your Japan itinerary.
